concept: Bake / Cook by Surrounding Dry Heat
description: To cook food through surrounding dry heat, often within an enclosed heated place. Bread is the familiar case, though the scope also includes roots, fruit, dough, and other foods. 'therapi' (boil) works through moving liquid; 'napako' lets heat reach the food from the chamber around it.
sound_symbolism: Dental 'na' opens into the stressed lip closure and broad vowel of 'pa'. To the ear, the opening is exactly 'napa' (bread). Final 'ko' closes at the velum and rounds the word into a deeper chamber.
grammatical_notes: A transitive verb: 'mia napa napako.' (I bake bread.) Nearby phrases identify the heat source, vessel, duration, and exact temperature. The event-noun rule supplies baking.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: A communal oven gathered several households around one firing, with each loaf read by color, smell, and the practiced hand.

semantic_domains.activity: cooking food through surrounding dry heat
semantic_domains.creation: turning dough or other food into a baked result
semantic_domains.physical: heat reaching food from an enclosing chamber
